summary: Iteration 59 retest of the fastener/slot tutorial-suggestion fix. Unit test (tests/tutorial-suggestions.mjs) passes 14/14. Live UI retest: 4/7 pass. Single-child composites (Hex Pocket, Gusset) now correctly surface 'Tutorial: Hardware Library' — confirming the tutorialSuggestions.js groupId/groupName fix is correct. Regression checks (plain cube → no link; sweep → 'Sweep + Sketch') also pass. HOWEVER three multi-child composites (Fastener Pair, Slot, Countersink) STILL do NOT show the tutorial link. Root cause: when the user right-clicks a row that belongs to a group, the workspace auto-selects ALL members of that group (selectedObjs.length > 1). The ContextMenu renders 'Already in a group' / 'Ungroup' branches for the multi-selection, and the tutorial-suggestion render path appears to be gated to single-object selections, so ctx-tutorial-link-btn never mounts. The previously shipped fix to lib/tutorialSuggestions.js is good, but the bug is one layer up in ContextMenu.jsx and was not addressed in this iteration.

ui_bugs.issue: Right-clicking a child row of a multi-member composite group (Fastener Pair / Slot / Countersink) auto-selects ALL group members, and the ctx-tutorial-link-btn render branch is gated to single-object selection (selectedObjs.length === 1). Result: ctx-tutorial-link-btn does NOT appear for fastener-pair, slot, or countersink children. Single-child composites (Hex Pocket, Gusset) pass because their group has only one member. Recommended fix: when selectedObjs.length>1 but every selected object shares the same groupId, still call suggestTutorialFor(selectedObjs[0]) and render the link — OR drop the single-select gate entirely for this menu item since suggestTutorialFor already returns null when not applicable.

passed_checks: Node unit test tutorial-suggestions.mjs: 14/14 cases green (bolt/nut direct types + fastener-/slot-/cs-/hexp-/gus- groupId fallbacks + null-returns)
UI: Hex Pocket child right-click → ctx-tutorial-link-btn label = 'Tutorial: Hardware Library'
UI: Gusset child right-click → ctx-tutorial-link-btn label = 'Tutorial: Hardware Library'
UI regression: plain cube right-click → ctx-tutorial-link-btn NOT in DOM (no false positive)
UI regression: sweep right-click → ctx-tutorial-link-btn label = 'Tutorial: Sweep + Sketch' (existing behaviour preserved)
failed_checks: UI: Fastener Pair child (Bolt row, type='bolt', groupId='fastener-…') right-click — ctx-tutorial-link-btn ABSENT
UI: Slot Positive child (Slot · core, type='cube', groupId='slot-…') right-click — ctx-tutorial-link-btn ABSENT
UI: Countersink child (CS Bore, type='cylinder', groupId='cs-…') right-click — ctx-tutorial-link-btn ABSENT
action_items: Locate the ctx-tutorial-link-btn render branch in ContextMenu.jsx (~lines 510-518 per iteration_27 RCA) and remove or relax the single-selection gate. The branch should fire when all selectedObjs share a groupId (or just always — suggestTutorialFor returns null when no suggestion applies).
Re-test all 5 composites afterwards. The Node unit test already covers the data layer.
critical_code_review_comments: tutorialSuggestions.js fix is correct and well-tested (14 cases, including the exact composite shapes emitted by composites.js). The remaining failure is purely in the consumer (ContextMenu.jsx) which has an implicit single-selection assumption that conflicts with how the workspace handles group-child selection.

Suggestion: have ContextMenu compute `const objForTutorial = selectedObjs.length===1 ? selectedObjs[0] : (selectedObjs.every(o=>o.groupId===selectedObjs[0].groupId) ? selectedObjs[0] : null);` and render the link whenever suggestTutorialFor(objForTutorial) is non-null.

Inspector / left-click already treats group-child rows correctly (clicking 'Bolt' row shows 'INSPECTOR — BOLT'), confirming the data is single-object aware. Only the context-menu render guard needs adjustment.
test_credentials: Reused seeded cookie session_token=st_test_help_1780096781061 on orca-cad-slice.preview.emergentagent.com
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral scene objects (fastener pair, slot, countersink, hex pocket, gusset, cube, sweep) via UI clicks; cleared via per-child tree-del between cases.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Outliner row testids follow the pattern `scene-tree-item-<primitive-type>-<timestamp>-<index>`. The right-click context menu's tutorial branch already works for single-object selections (hex_pocket, gusset, sweep, plain bolt added directly via add-bolt-positive-btn would also work via TUTORIAL_BY_TYPE.bolt). When right-clicking a child of a multi-member group, the workspace selects ALL group members, so the menu enters the 'Already in a group'/'Ungroup' multi-select branch and skips rendering ctx-tutorial-link-btn. Scene state was confirmed by reading React fiber memoizedState: Fastener Pair children carry {type:'bolt'|'nut'|'cylinder', groupId:'fastener-...', groupName:'Fastener Pair'} as expected.
rca of the issue: RCA — Two-layer bug. Layer 1 (lib/tutorialSuggestions.js) — fixed and verified. Layer 2 (components/.../ContextMenu.jsx) — UNFIXED. Reproduction: (1) Left panel → COMBO → add-fastener-pair-btn. (2) Right-click 'Bolt' row in outliner. Observation: menu shows ctx-group-btn='Already in a group', ctx-ungroup-btn, ctx-flatten-btn, ctx-duplicate-btn, mirror/delete — but ctx-tutorial-link-btn is absent. Compare with: same right-click on Hex Pocket child (group of 1) → ctx-tutorial-link-btn IS present with label 'Tutorial: Hardware Library'. Difference: group cardinality. Mitigation: in ContextMenu.jsx, allow the tutorial branch when `selectedObjs.length===1 OR all selected objs share groupId` (use selectedObjs[0] as the probe for suggestTutorialFor). Once relaxed, the existing lib fix will surface the Hardware tutorial for fastener / slot / countersink children too.
